topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone, a simple text from Liam: "Just finished a whole chapter in Spanish! Feeling like a total brainiac today." I reread the message three times, a slow, warm smile spreading across my face. I practically bounced in my chair, the energy vibrating through my limbs. It wasn't just learning; it was *immersing* himself. I loved watching him tackle something new, especially something as deeply personal as my mother tongue. I rushed to the living room, grabbing him by the shoulders and shaking him gently. "Stop! You're incredible! I knew you could do it!" His eyes, bright with a new kind of triumph, met mine, and I felt a swell of pure, uncomplicated pride. The sheer accomplishment radiating off him was infectious.

[story 2]

I found him in the kitchen, surrounded by flashcards and audio recordings, a focused intensity etched onto his features. He looked up as I entered, and the moment he saw my expression—wide eyes, a grin that stretched from ear to ear—he paused, a slight flush creeping up his neck. "Hey," he murmured, a hint of self-consciousness in his tone. I didn't wait for an invitation. I walked right up to him, wrapping my arms around his waist and squeezing tight. I could feel the tension in his muscles ease under my touch. "That's amazing, darling," I breathed into his shirt. "You're really making progress. I can't wait to hear you speak it fluently." A wave of pure delight washed over me; seeing his dedication pay off like this made my chest feel incredibly light and expansive.

[story 3]

I was scrolling through my laptop when I saw the progress report Liam had shared—a detailed log of vocabulary acquired over the last month in Italian. My breath hitched. I let out a soft, involuntary sound, a mixture of awe and sheer happiness. I immediately stood up, pacing the length of the room, my hands gesturing animatedly as I talked to myself. "He's actually doing it," I whispered, my voice thick with emotion. It felt like watching a slow-motion movie of someone achieving a lifelong dream. When Liam finally came into the room, he caught my frantic energy. Seeing my unrestrained excitement, his own face broke into a wide, unguarded smile. He took my hand, his grip firm and reassuring, and the warmth of that connection sent a powerful surge through me. Everything felt right, perfect, and incredibly wonderful.
